steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Find which of the roman-numeral statements (I: $x = a$, II: $x = 24$, III: $x = 25$) are solutions to $x - a = (x - a)(x - 24)$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
Rearrange: $(x - a) - (x - a)(x - 24) = 0$, so $(x - a)[1 - (x - 24)] = 0$, giving $(x - a)(25 - x) = 0$. The solutions are $x = a$ and $x = 25$.
  • Statement I — '$x = a$': $x = a$ is a root. ✓
  • Statement II — '$x = 24$': Not a root. Plugging in: LHS $= 24 - a eq 0$ (since $a > 25$), RHS $= (24 - a)(0) = 0$. ✗
  • Statement III — '$x = 25$': $x = 25$ is a root. ✓
I and III only — that matches choice B — B is correct.
